content: Hi, I'm Spencer and I'm a Veterinarian at a rural practice. I've been recovering from a knee injury and I'm trying to adjust my routines to accommodate my new limitations.
Sorry to hear that you're dealing with a knee injury. Can you tell me a bit more about how it's affected your daily life?
Well, I've had to cut back on my physical activities, which has been tough for me. I used to love hiking and biking, but now I have to stick to lower-impact activities like knitting and board games.
It's great that you're finding ways to adapt to your new limitations. Have you had to make any changes to your work schedule or routines?
Yes, I've had to adjust my work schedule to avoid any activities that might exacerbate my injury. I've also had to hire some extra help at the practice to cover for me while I'm recovering.
That makes sense. Have you had to make any changes to your budget to accommodate your new expenses and reduced income?
Yes, I've had to cut back on some of my discretionary spending, like dining out and travel. I've also had to allocate more money to my medical expenses and physical therapy.
I understand. It can be challenging to adjust to a new financial situation. Can you tell me a bit more about your current budget and expenses?

My monthly expenses are around $3,500, which includes my rent, utilities, and medical expenses. I've also been trying to save up for a big purchase - a new car, which I hope to buy by the end of the year.
That's a good goal. Have you considered setting up a separate savings fund for the car purchase?
Yes, I've been trying to save $1,000 per month for the car. I'm hoping to have enough money by the end of December.
That's a good plan. It looks like you have a clear idea of what you want to achieve. Now, let's talk about your income. How much do you expect to earn per month as a Veterinarian?
I expect to earn around $5,000 per month, although that may vary depending on the number of clients I see and the services I provide.
Okay, that helps. It looks like you have a good idea of your income and expenses. I think we can work out a budget that will help you achieve your goals while accommodating your new limitations.
